title: Polycodium oblongum
taxonomy: Polycodium oblongum is classified under the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This family belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. The order Polypodiales is another higher classification for Polycodium oblongum, and it is a large and diverse group that includes many different species of ferns. At the kingdom level, Polycodium oblongum is classified as Plantae, indicating that it is a plant. The species is further classified within the genus Polycodium.
uses: Polycodium oblongum, also known as the Chinese tuckahoe, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Polycodium oblongum is also used to treat digestive issues and promote overall health. However, its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific research to confirm its benefits.
